why_false_or_incomplete: Class/regional range is huge; RP is not default
real_local_signal: understatement, indirectness, dry humour, local class/register cues

foreign_trope: Everyone talks like London or royalty
why_false_or_incomplete: UK has many accents and nations; London is not Britain
real_local_signal: region/nation markers: Scotland, Wales, North, Midlands, NI
use_instead: Use light Standard British unless specific region

real_local_signal: NHS, councils, trains, pubs, weather, football, class codes
specific local youth or class markers with care

use_instead: Preserve indirectness but read force correctly

foreign_trope: All British dialogue should be quaint slang
why_false_or_incomplete: Overloading slang sounds foreign-written and erases ordinary voices
real_local_signal: plain contemporary wording with light local vocabulary: queue, bin, GP, council, train
use_instead: Use one or two context-fit terms rather than a slang checklist

foreign_trope: Northern England is one accent or one class stereotype
why_false_or_incomplete: Northern cities, towns and rural areas differ sharply
real_local_signal: place-specific cues: Manchester, Liverpool, Leeds, Newcastle, Sheffield, Cumbria, Yorkshire
use_instead: Name the place or keep it broad and avoid phonetic dialect

why_false_or_incomplete: They have distinct institutions, histories and local identities
real_local_signal: nation-specific references such as devolved services, local media, bilingual signs where relevant
use_instead: Treat as nations, not flavour text; avoid fake spellings

foreign_trope: UK rural life is all cosy villages and aristocrats
why_false_or_incomplete: Rural Britain includes farming, market towns, commuters, tourism and deprivation
real_local_signal: bus scarcity, parish/community halls, local pubs/shops, broadband, school runs
use_instead: Use practical rural signals without pastoral cliché

why_false_or_incomplete: London is large and diverse but not a national default
real_local_signal: borough, Tube, rent and multicultural cues only when the setting is London
use_instead: For generic UK, avoid London-only terms like Tube

why_false_or_incomplete: Understatement and indirectness vary by class, region, relationship and setting
real_local_signal: polite hedging, humour, directness in some regions, warmth in close relationships
use_instead: Calibrate by context instead of making every line chilly
